# .env template - Secure environment variables
# Generated by Security Audit System on {timestamp}

# Database Configuration
# DB_PASSWORD={generated_var_name}
# DB_URL=postgresql://user:{generated_var_name}@localhost:5432/dbname

# API Keys
# API_KEY={generated_var_name}
# SECRET_KEY={generated_var_name}

# Authentication
# JWT_SECRET={generated_var_name}
# AUTH_TOKEN={generated_var_name}

# Cloud Provider Credentials
# AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID={generated_var_name}
# AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY={generated_var_name}

# Instructions:
# 1. Replace {generated_var_name} placeholders with actual secret values
# 2. Update your application code to use os.environ.get() or python-dotenv
# 3. Add .env to .gitignore if not already present
# 4. Never commit .env files to version control
# 5. Use .env.example for team sharing (without actual secrets)
